# spec-0.9 D-5 / T-7: dry-run output FORMAT reference
#
# This file documents the EXPECTED FORMAT of `sync-branch-protection.sh --dry-run`.
# It is NOT used for byte-level diff in CI because:
#
#   - The `config:` line shows the absolute path (varies by machine)
#   - The `repo:` line shows the inferred GH repo (varies by fork)
#   - The `current required_status_checks.contexts:` section reflects live GH
#     state — it changes after T-8 cutover and during legacy-job deletion (T-9).
#
# Real CI gating is via `make ci-script-check` (tools/ci-protection-check),
# which enforces the structural invariant "ci.yml stable-name jobs == JSON
# contexts" without depending on machine-specific output.
#
# See spec-0.9-ci-github-actions.md § 2.4 + § 4.3 TC-12.

=== Plan ===
repo:   <owner>/<repo>
config: <abs-path>/scripts/ci/branch-protection-required-checks.json
mode:   dry-run

current required_status_checks.contexts:
  - <whatever GH protection currently lists, one per line, sorted by GH>

target required_status_checks.contexts:
  + validate
  + build-linux
  + build-macos
  + unit-test
  + import-rules
  + dependency-policy
  + cli-golden
  + security
  + perf-smoke

[dry-run] would PATCH /repos/<owner>/<repo>/branches/main/protection/required_status_checks
          (run with --apply to write)
